target: WIN-062 detail_verdict_text — verify ISS-1258 (carve-out undersell, c5 H0) is OBE: the hole was addressed by decider self-apply commit d4dc3a54 (2026-04-20T22:38) closing the parallel ISS-1232 (c4 H4). Current text contains the recommended condition-1 sentence; no further patch needed.
review_class_override_note: Tracker classified as deep-attack (baby-orphan default, class_hint=deep-attack from PROP-027 routing). Authored EXP declares verification: the work is a current-text audit confirming an already-applied patch (d4dc3a54, ISS-1232) has resolved the carve-out-undersell hole that ISS-1258 also tracks. No new patch; no new arguments; recommended action is close-as-already-fixed-by-source. Per PROP-025: "verification that prior decider patch closed parallel issue" → verification.
scope_summary: ISS-1258 (severity=minor, status=assigned-analyst) tracks WIN-062.c5 H0 "carve-out undersell" — c5 review noted that the rewritten paragraph 2 of detail_verdict_text invoked only Part 3.1b condition (2) (not-plausibly-known-at-write-time) while the carve-out has three conjunctive conditions. Curmudgeon recommendation (verbatim from WIN-062.c5.json, holes_found[0]): "Optional: append a half-sentence to the final sentence, e.g. '... a century-old published velocity does not qualify — and the derivation is not unique to the dome either, since the disc_diameter was itself fitted to globe circumference.' One sentence closes both legs." The same hole was also tracked as ISS-1232 (c4 H4, found 2026-04-20T20:31). Decider self-apply d4dc3a54 (2026-04-20T22:38, commit message "Decider self-apply: 6 patches ... WIN-062 carve-out") appended the following sentence to detail_verdict_text closing ISS-1232: "The derivation condition (Part 3.1b, condition 1) also fails independently: the dome re-expresses Tesla's measured value using disc diameter rather than globe circumference, producing numerical agreement by construction rather than independent derivation from dome geometry." That sentence remains in the current text (verified 2026-05-16 by reading wins.json id='062' detail_verdict_text on FUSE). Same hole, same fix; ISS-1258 should close as already-fixed-by-source.
kernel_of_truth: The c5 H0 curmudgeon hole was substantive at the time it was written (2026-04-20T20:33) — the then-current detail_verdict_text really did cite only condition (2) of the three-condition Part 3.1b carve-out, and a literalist defender could legitimately point out "you've only shown one leg of the test fails." The recommendation was correct; the fix was applied 2 hours later via a parallel issue (ISS-1232) the decider self-classified as patchable. ISS-1258 was created 12 minutes BEFORE the patch landed (22:25 vs 22:37) and never got re-routed once the parallel fix shipped — a classic stale-issue-from-overlapping-cycles bug. The carve-out condition (3) ("derivation-plus-validation, not just discovery") is technically a separate leg, but condition (3) is a stricter form of condition (2) — a claim that fails (2) (data plausibly known to author) automatically fails (3) (no pre-registered derivation-plus-validation in the timestamped document, since there was no contemporaneous derivation at all). The current text's explicit citation of conditions (1) and (2) is therefore sufficient: (1) closes the uniqueness leg via the disc-diameter-fitted-to-globe argument, (2) closes the knowledge leg via the 1899/1978 publication chronology, and (3) collapses into (2) without separate citation.
holes_addressed: WIN-062.c5 H0 (minor): "Carve-out undersell — Rewritten paragraph 2 invokes only Part 3.1b condition (2). Citing only (2) understates the case and preserves a small defender opening." — closed by decider self-apply commit d4dc3a54 (2026-04-20T22:38), which appended the condition-1 sentence the curmudgeon recommended. Verified verbatim in current data/wins.json id='062' detail_verdict_text (FUSE read 2026-05-16, this run).

anticipated_objections.objection: Closing ISS-1258 as OBE leaves no analyst-side audit trail for the parallel ISS-1232/ISS-1258 routing bug — the decoupling will keep recurring (parallel issues for the same hole found in c4 and c5 cycles).
anticipated_objections.response: True; the structural fix belongs in tinker's de-duplication backlog, not in this EXP. Filing this as an explicit OBE-close with full chronology (ISS-1232 found 20:31, ISS-1258 found 22:25, d4dc3a54 patch 22:37) gives the decider's review pass and any future tinker audit the data trail needed to spot the pattern. The EXP itself is the audit artifact. PROP-029 safety net (orphan ISS detection in Mode 1 dispatcher) already covers the routing pathology at the open-issues end; this is a different shape (parallel-issues-for-same-hole) and warrants its own tinker proposal if it recurs.

anticipated_objections.objection: Condition (3) of the Part 3.1b carve-out is not explicitly cited in the current detail_verdict_text. A pedantic defender could press: "You cite conditions (1) and (2) but not (3); you have not shown the test fails on all three legs."
anticipated_objections.response: Condition (3) ("derivation-plus-validation in the timestamped document, not just discovery") is a stricter form of condition (2). A claim that fails (2) — data plausibly known to author at write-time — automatically fails (3), because (3) requires the author to have published the derivation AND the predicted value together BEFORE the observation arrived. Tesla's measurement was published decades before the dome adopted it (1899 measurement, 1978 posthumous Beograd publication), so the dome could not have pre-registered a derivation-plus-validation at any later date — the validation step is the dome adopting an already-published number. The current text's chronology paragraph and condition-(1) construction-by-fitting argument together cover the condition-(3) territory implicitly. Adding an explicit condition-(3) sentence would lengthen the paragraph by ~30 words for one literalist defender — not worth the prose cost. WONTFIX recommendation for any follow-up. (If a future cycle pushes back on this, a 12-word append like "and condition (3) — pre-registered derivation-plus-validation — fails for the same reason" closes that leg too.)

summary_for_decider: EXP-403 is an OBE finding for ISS-1258 (WIN-062.c5 H0 carve-out undersell, severity=minor, status=assigned-analyst, age 26d). The parallel ISS-1232 (c4 H4, same hole) was closed by decider self-apply commit d4dc3a54 on 2026-04-20T22:38, which appended the recommended condition-1 sentence to detail_verdict_text. The fix remains in current text (verified on FUSE 2026-05-16). ISS-1258 was created 12 minutes before d4dc3a54 landed and never got re-routed once the parallel fix shipped — a stale-issue artifact of overlapping curmudgeon c4/c5 cycles on the same WIN. Recommended action: close ISS-1258 as already-fixed-by-source (cite d4dc3a54 in the closure note). No patch needed; no verdict change; no new arguments. Carve-out condition (3) is technically uncited in current text but collapses into condition (2) — WONTFIX for any follow-up unless a defender presses literalism. After this EXP integrates, the WIN-062.c5 hole inventory drops H0; remaining H1-H6 are pre-existing minor carry-forwards (H1 sourcing-window phrasing, H2 OTS rebuttal preemption — already addressed by current text's Timestamp Error cross-link; H3-H6 cross-WIN consistency carry-forwards batched with ISS-1087).
